_meta.description: Land Tax Value Stream Map — Complete Data Specification

arrays.DATA: 6 value stages with Land Tax L3 capabilities

DATA.description: Maintain an accurate and current representation of land and ownership so all Land Tax decisions are based on reliable information.

l2.name: Taxable Land Maintenance
l2.description: Maintain records of land that is subject to Land Tax, ensuring only qualifying land is included in assessments.

l2.name: Subdivisions & Consolidations
l2.description: Manage changes to land parcels resulting from subdivision or consolidation, and update taxable land records accordingly.

l2.name: Title to Property Relationship
l2.description: Maintain the relationship between legal land titles and property records, ensuring title changes are reflected in the system.

l2.name: Ownership Maintenance
l2.description: Maintain ownership details and ownership changes over time so the correct party is held liable.

l2.name: Joint Ownership
l2.description: Manage land owned by more than one party, including proportional interests and lead-owner assignment.

l2.name: Land Held on Trust
l2.description: Manage land ownership where land is held under trust arrangements, capturing trustee and beneficiary relationships.

l2.name: Unknown Customer
l2.description: Manage land or ownership records where the liable party is not yet identified, flagging for investigation.

DATA.stage: VS2 – Determine How Land Is Taxed
DATA.description: Determine the correct Land Tax treatment for land based on ownership structure and circumstances.

l2.name: Joint Ownership
l2.description: Determine tax treatment for land owned jointly, applying the correct grouping and rate rules.

l2.name: Land Held on Trust
l2.description: Determine tax treatment for land held under trust arrangements, applying trust-specific rates and surcharges.

l2.name: Determine Land Tax Liability
l2.description: Determine whether Land Tax applies to specific land and how liability is calculated, including thresholds and exemptions.

l2.name: Exemption Creation Management
l2.description: Defining and configuring exemption types available under the Land Tax Act, including eligibility criteria and duration.

l3.name: Automated Exemption Application
l3.description: System-driven application of an exemption based on configured business rules and data triggers.

l3.name: Manual Exemption Application
l3.description: Processing a customer-submitted application for an exemption by a staff member.

l2.name: Concession Application
l2.description: Receiving and processing applications for land tax concessions including eligibility assessment and approval.

l2.name: Tax/Entitlement Rate Management
l2.description: Managing the land tax rates applicable to different taxpayer types and ownership categories.

l3.name: Tax Free Threshold Management
l3.description: Managing the tax-free threshold below which no land tax is payable.

l3.name: Absentee Owner Management
l3.description: Managing the identification and assessment of absentee owners who are subject to additional land tax surcharge.

l3.name: Build to Rent Management
l3.description: Managing the registration and assessment of build-to-rent properties subject to specific land tax treatment.

l2.name: Usage Management
l2.description: Managing information about how a property is occupied and used, which affects land tax exemption and concession eligibility.

DATA.description: Calculate Land Tax liabilities and issue formal assessments to customers.

l2.name: Valuations Maintenance
l2.description: Maintain valuation data used for Land Tax assessment, including updates from the Valuer-General.

l2.name: General Valuations
l2.description: Apply standard Valuer-General valuation data for the annual Land Tax assessment cycle.
l2.policy: Land Tax Act 2005 — s21; Part 1 definitions

l2.name: Supplementary Valuations
l2.description: Apply updated valuations when land circumstances change between general valuation cycles.

l2.name: SRO Requested Valuations
l2.description: Manage valuations specifically requested by SRO to support assessment decisions.

l2.name: Single Valuation for Multiple Properties
l2.description: Apply a single valuation across related properties where appropriate under the Land Tax Act.

l2.name: Generate Land Tax Assessments
l2.description: Generate Land Tax assessment outcomes using validated liability calculations and valuation data.

l2.name: Issue Land Tax Assessments
l2.description: Issue formal Land Tax assessments to customers, giving legal effect to the tax obligation.

l3.name: Issue Cycle Schedule Management
l3.description: Scheduling and managing the timing of the annual land tax issue cycle.

l3.name: First Time Liability Management
l3.description: Managing the identification and assessment of taxpayers newly liable for land tax.

l3.name: Automated Liability/Entitlement Calculation
l3.description: System-driven automated calculation of land tax liability during the issue cycle.

l3.name: Manual Liability/Entitlement Calculation
l3.description: Performing a manual land tax calculation in response to specific circumstances or amendments.

l3.name: Trust Deduction Management
l3.description: Managing the trust deduction available to certain trust structures under the Land Tax Act.

l3.name: Minimum Liability Threshold Management
l3.description: Managing the minimum liability amount below which no assessment is issued.

DATA.description: Settle Land Tax liabilities through payments, refunds, instalments, and adjustments.

l2.name: Payment Management
l2.description: Receive and manage Land Tax payments from customers across all payment channels.

l2.name: Payment Allocation Management
l2.description: Allocate payments to the correct Land Tax liabilities at customer and property levels.

l2.name: Instalment Management
l2.description: Manage instalment arrangements for paying Land Tax, including schedule setup and monitoring.

l2.name: Refund Management
l2.description: Process refunds arising from overpayment or reassessment of Land Tax.

l2.name: Adjustment Management
l2.description: Apply financial adjustments to Land Tax liabilities following reassessment, amendment, or correction.

l3.name: Property Liability Allocation
l3.description: Allocating payments against liabilities at the property level.

DATA.description: Resolve objections and disputes and ensure compliance with Land Tax obligations.

l2.name: Objection Management
l2.description: Manage formal objections to Land Tax decisions, including receipt, review and determination.

l2.name: Valuation Objection Management
l2.description: Manage objections relating specifically to land valuations used in Land Tax assessments.

l2.name: Dispute Resolution Management
l2.description: Resolve disputes arising from Land Tax matters, including VCAT referrals and settlements.

l2.name: Case Management
l2.description: Manage Land Tax cases across their full lifecycle including creation, tracking and closure.

l2.name: Compliance Management
l2.description: Monitor and address non-compliance with Land Tax obligations, including proactive and reactive activities.

l2.name: Audit Management
l2.description: Conduct audit activities to validate correctness and compliance with land tax obligations.

l2.name: Risk Management
l2.description: Identify and manage risks associated with Land Tax administration.

l2.name: Quality Assurance Management
l2.description: Ensure accuracy and consistency of Land Tax decisions through sampling and review activities.

l3.name: Private Ruling Management
l3.description: Managing applications for private rulings on land tax obligations.

DATA.description: Provide trusted evidence of Land Tax status relied on by customers and third parties.

l2.name: Issue Land Tax Certificates
l2.description: Issue certificates confirming Land Tax status for land, used in property settlement and conveyancing.

l2.name: Certificate Evidence & Record Management
l2.description: Maintain records and evidence supporting issued certificates, including liability outstanding at issue.

l2.name: Assessment Cancellation Management
l2.desc: Managing the cancellation of assessment notices that have been issued in error or superseded.

l2.name: Assessment Generation Management
l2.desc: Managing the process of generating assessment notices from calculated liabilities.

l2.name: Assessment Withdrawal Management
l2.desc: Managing the formal withdrawal of issued assessment notices.

l2.name: Service of Notice Management
l2.desc: Managing the formal service of assessment notices to customers, including electronic and postal channels.

l3s.name: Objection Creation Management
l3s.description: Creating new objection records in response to a customer disputing a land tax assessment.

l2.name: Case Creation
l2.desc: Creating new cases to track complex land tax matters, private rulings and referrals.

l3s.name: Formal Objection Management
l3s.description: Managing the referral and tracking of formal objections through to determination.

l3s.name: Self Reporting Management
l3s.description: Managing the receipt and processing of self-reported information from taxpayers.

l3s.name: Tip Offs and Voluntary Disclosure Management
l3s.description: Managing anonymous and named tip-offs from third parties regarding suspected non-compliance.

l3s.name: Potential Investigation Cases (PICs)
l3s.description: Identifying and managing cases where a taxpayer may not be meeting their land tax obligations.

l2.name: Insolvency Relationship Management
l2.desc: Managing land tax obligations and debt recovery for customers who are subject to insolvency proceedings.

l2.name: Automated Communication Management
l2.desc: Managing system-generated outbound communications triggered by assessment events.

l3s.name: Electronic Communication Consent Management
l3s.description: Managing customer consent to receive official communications electronically.

l2.name: Customer Communication Record Management
l2.desc: Recording and maintaining a history of all communications with customers.

l3s.name: Automated Customer Creation
l3s.description: System-initiated creation of customer records based on data received from third-party sources such as land registries.

l3s.name: Manual Customer Creation
l3s.description: Creating a new customer record through direct staff data entry, typically in response to a customer contact or application.

l3s.name: Customer Data Matching
l3s.description: Comparing incoming customer data against existing records to identify potential matches and prevent duplicates.

l3s.name: Customer Identity Verification
l3s.description: Confirming a customer's identity against authoritative sources before granting access or making changes to their account.

l3s.name: Duplicate Customer Detection
l3s.description: Automated and manual processes to identify customer records that may represent the same real-world entity.

l3s.name: Joint Ownership Member Management
l3s.description: Managing all customers who share ownership of a property, including their proportional interests.

l3s.name: Trustee Management
l3s.description: Recording and maintaining trustee details and their relationship to trust accounts.

l2.name: Interest Management
l2.desc: Managing interest charges applied to overdue Land Tax liabilities.

l2.name: Penalty Management
l2.desc: Managing penalties applied for late lodgement or payment of Land Tax.

l2.name: Write Off Management
l2.desc: Managing the write-off of Land Tax debts deemed irrecoverable.

l3s.name: Customer Liability Allocation
l3s.description: Allocating payments against liabilities at the customer level.

l2.name: Suspense Management
l2.desc: Managing unallocated or unidentified payments held in suspense pending correct allocation.

l2.name: Refund Data Management
l2.desc: Maintaining records of refund transactions and supporting documentation.

l2.name: Address Validation Management
l2.desc: Validating addresses against authoritative sources such as GNAF to ensure they are accurate and deliverable.

l2.name: Property Creation
l2.desc: Creating new property records in the system from data received via third-party feeds or manual staff entry.

l3s.name: Property Data Validation
l3s.description: Applying business rules to confirm property data meets mandatory format and integrity requirements.

l2.name: Ownership Management
l2.desc: Managing the link between properties and their registered owners within the revenue system.

l2.name: Subdivision/Consolidation Management
l2.desc: Managing property record changes arising from subdivision or consolidation events.

l3s.name: Automatic Revenue Line Registration Creation
l3s.description: System-triggered creation of land tax registrations based on data events such as property ownership changes.

l2.name: Valuation Data Acquisition
l2.desc: Receiving and ingesting valuation data from the Valuer-General and other authoritative sources.

l2.name: Valuation Data Management
l2.desc: Maintaining the accuracy and completeness of valuation records used in assessment calculations.

items.desc: Managing the automated processing of land tax transactions based on configured business rules.
Managing scheduled batch jobs that drive bulk processing of assessments, payments and communications.
Governing the lifecycle of business rules including approval, versioning and retirement.
Configuring business rules that drive automated validation, eligibility and processing decisions.
Managing exceptions raised when automated processing cannot complete due to data or rule failures.
Managing the physical and logical storage of documents and content associated with land tax records.
Ensuring content is stored, published and accessible to authorised staff and customers.
Integrating content storage systems with the core revenue system for seamless document linkage.
Managing the impact of legislative changes on land tax processes and system configuration.
Creating, maintaining and communicating policies governing land tax administration.
Creating and maintaining operational procedures that guide staff in consistent execution of processes.
Managing organisational change initiatives including communications, training and impact assessment.
Developing and managing the strategic plan for land tax operations and capability improvement.
Managing contracts and agreements with third-party service providers.
Classifying customer data by its sensitivity level to determine appropriate handling, access and storage requirements.
Managing the storage, access and use of personally identifiable information in compliance with privacy laws.
Conducting audits to ensure customer data access and usage complies with privacy policies and legislation.
Identifying and assessing potential privacy breaches to determine their scope and severity.
Issuing mandatory notifications to affected individuals and regulators following a confirmed privacy breach.
Correcting or removing compromised data following a privacy breach.
Creating and maintaining written guidance materials to help customers understand and meet their obligations.
Providing digital calculators and interactive tools to help customers self-assess their obligations.
Delivering education through interactive formats such as webinars, workshops or online help.
Collecting, recording and acting on feedback from customers about their service experience.
Maintaining previous versions of records to enable historical comparison and audit.
Recording all actions taken by users within the system against customer and revenue records.
Managing associations between history records and related entities.
Managing procedures and policy documents within the knowledge management system.
Delivering compulsory and role-based training to land tax staff.
Providing staff with guidance and escalation support for complex land tax enquiries.
Managing skills analysis and training plans to support succession and capability planning.
Planning and managing team resources and capacity for land tax operations.
Identifying and implementing continuous improvements to land tax processes.
Managing staff engagement, feedback and development within operational teams.
Managing meeting agendas, minutes and follow-up actions for operational governance meetings.
Using digital collaboration platforms to support team communication and document sharing.
Setting, tracking and reporting on KPIs for land tax processing and service delivery.
Setting, tracking and managing SLAs for land tax service delivery.
Managing quality standards and measures for land tax processing outputs.
Measuring performance against corporate and branch-level KPIs and strategic objectives.
Managing individual staff performance through scorecards and development plans.
Managing formal audit activities including quality assurance audits and website content reviews.
Sampling and validating assessments and decisions to ensure accuracy and compliance with policy.
Identifying, assessing and mitigating risks through standardised decision-making tools.
Ensuring the quality and integrity of system configurations and processing outputs.
Producing reports on land tax processing volumes, automation rates and workload.
Producing reports on land tax revenue collections, forecasts and accruals.
Producing financial reports including income and expenditure reports for the land tax program.
Producing reports on compliance with land tax obligations and internal administration standards.
Analysing land tax data to identify trends, insights and areas for improvement.
Producing reports for external stakeholders including Treasury and Department of Treasury and Finance.
Forecasting future land tax workloads and workforce requirements.
Managing the tools and platforms used for reporting and data visualisation.
Managing staff access to land tax systems, including role-based access profiles.
Managing external user access including customer and agent portal access.
Managing the secure transfer of data files between the revenue system and external parties.
Proactively engaging with stakeholders to inform, consult and collaborate.
Maintaining formal agreements with stakeholders such as MOUs and data sharing arrangements.
Delivering training, conferences and briefings to stakeholders.
Managing relationships and communications with internal business units and cross-divisional teams.
Managing the design standards for customer and agent-facing digital systems.
Managing the design standards for internal staff-facing systems.
Managing system quality testing and user acceptance testing processes before deployment.
Managing aggregated views of data to provide a consolidated picture of customer and revenue data.
Managing the configuration of business alerts surfaced within the system to prompt staff action.
Creating workflow tasks either manually by staff or automatically triggered by system events.
Assigning workflow tasks to individual staff or team queues.
Automating the closure and processing of workflow tasks based on configured business rules.
Managing priority flags and escalation rules to ensure high-priority tasks are actioned promptly.
Tracking and managing service level agreements for workflow task completion.
Tracking key performance indicators related to workflow processing efficiency.
Managing flags that suppress workflow creation or assessment issuance for certain customers or registrations.
